meta.note: Ground-marker layer: where to reliably see wildlife and do things. Complements (does not repeat) claude-lore.json. Curated 2026-07-05.

items.name: Whale watching from shore
items.description: Humpbacks pass through Sutil Channel spring through fall — watch from the Smelt Bay to Sutil Point shoreline; blows are visible on calm days, occasionally orcas in transit.

items.name: Great blue herons — Mansons Lagoon
items.description: Herons stalk the lagoon flats at low tide year-round; bring binoculars and check the spit at dawn.

items.name: Shorebirds on Shark Spit
items.description: Black oystercatchers, plovers and gulls work the sand of Marina Island's spit; boaters picnic mid-spit and walk the tideline.

items.name: Eagles over Bullock Bluff
items.description: Bald eagles ride the updrafts where Sutil and Lewis channels meet at the island's north tip.

items.name: Winter waterfowl — Gunflint Lake
items.description: Loons, buffleheads and goldeneyes raft on the lake through the cold months; the Kw'as Park shoreline trail is the viewing platform.

items.name: Kayak: Whaletown to the pictographs
items.description: Classic half-day paddle from the Whaletown dock through Uganda Passage to the Gorge Harbour pictograph cliff — go at mid-tide, hug the west wall slowly.

items.name: Stargazing — Smelt Bay beach
items.description: No streetlights and a clear southwest horizon over the Salish Sea make Smelt Bay the island's default dark-sky beach; the Milky Way is routine on moonless nights.

items.name: Salmon in Basil Creek
items.description: Chum salmon return to Basil Creek at Squirrel Cove in late fall; the Klahoose Nation and streamkeepers monitor the run.
